model.label: MiMo-V2-Pro
model.description: MiMo • The flagship foundation for the agentic era, built for complex, long-horizon, multi-step agentic tasks.

pick.rationale: Arizona is the #1 seed in the West with a 31-2 record and ranks #3 in both KenPom and Torvik ratings, featuring elite offense (rank 7) and defense (rank 3). LIU, a 16-seed from the NEC, has a KenPom rank of 216 and lacks the talent to compete with Arizona's depth and athleticism. Despite Koa Peat's earlier injury, he returned for the Big 12 tournament and is expected to be available, making a 16-over-1 upset historically improbable and analytically implausible.

reasoningStep.title: West 1/16 Matchup Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Arizona is a dominant #1 seed facing a significant underdog in LIU, with massive disparities in team quality and tournament experience.
evidence: Arizona holds KenPom rank 3 and Torvik rank 3 with a 31-2 record, while LIU is KenPom 216 and Torvik 234 with a 24-10 record.
Arizona's adjusted efficiency margin (+37.56) dwarfs LIU's (-3.95), indicating a colossal talent gap.
Arizona's star freshman Koa Peat returned from injury in early March and played in the Big 12 tournament, alleviating major health concerns.
Historical data shows 16-seeds have only beaten 1-seeds once in NCAA tournament history, underscoring the extreme improbability of an LIU victory.

result.text: Arizona's Koa Peat Makes Impact in Return From Injury - Tucson Today Skip to content National Today Menu National Today Today Holidays Animals Arts & Entertainment Cause Cultural Federal Food & Beverage Health Relationships Religious Special Interest Birthdays January February March April May June July August September October November December Reminders Cities Atlanta Austin Baltimore Berwyn Beverly Hills Birmingham Boston Brooklyn Buffalo Charlotte Chicago Cincinnati Cleveland Columbus Dallas Denver Detroit Fort Worth Houston Indianapolis Knoxville Las Vegas Los Angeles Louisville Madison Memphis Miami Milwaukee Minneapolis Nashville New Orleans New York Omaha Orlando Philadelphia Phoenix Pittsburgh Portland Raleigh Richmond Rutherford Sacramento Salt Lake City San Antonio San Diego San Francisco San Jose Seattle Tampa Tucson Washington Log in Sign up Search Search Cancel Search National Today Submit Tucson Today By the People, for the People News Sports Basketball Baseball Body Building Boxing Cycling Equestrian eSports Extreme Sports Football Golf Gymnastics Hockey Ice Skating Mixed Martial Arts Motorsports Olympics Other Sports Rodeo Running Skiing Soccer Softball Surfing Swimming Tennis Track & Field Volleyball Wrestling More Arizona's Koa Peat Makes Impact in Return From Injury Wildcats cruise to 84-61 win over Kansas Jayhawks with Peat back in the lineup. Mar. 2, 2026 at 11:55pm Got story updates? Submit your updates here. › Arizona forward Koa Peat made his return in an emphatic way as the Wildcats cruised to an 84-61 win over the Kansas Jayhawks at home. Peat had missed Arizona's previous three games after suffering a lower body injury against Texas Tech on Feb. 14, but his return has raised the ceiling for one of the best teams in the country heading into the NCAA Tournament. Why it matters Peat's performance against Kansas is a positive sign for Arizona as they look to make a deep run in the NCAA Tournament. The Wildcats were able to survive his absence, but having Peat back at full strength makes them an even more dangerous team. The details Peat scored 12 points on 4-9 shooting, grabbed 7 rebounds, and dished out 5 assists in 23 minutes of action against Kansas. Head coach Tommy Lloyd spoke about the impact of having Peat back on the court, saying it's 'good to kind of get all the pieces back together' as they look to 'reform into something even better down the stretch of the season'. Peat suffered a lower body injury against Texas Tech on Feb. 14. Peat missed Arizona's previous three games before returning against Kansas on Feb. 28, 2026. Arizona has two regular season games remaining against Iowa State and Colorado. The players Koa Peat A freshman forward for the Arizona Wildcats who is averaging 13.8 points and 5.4 rebounds per game this season. result.text: Arizona's Koa Peat Makes Impact in Return From Injury - Tucson Today Skip to content National Today Menu National Today Today Holidays Animals Arts & Entertainment Cause Cultural Federal Food & Beverage Health Relationships Religious Special Interest Birthdays January February March April May June July August September October November December Reminders Cities Atlanta Austin Baltimore Berwyn Beverly Hills Birmingham Boston Brooklyn Buffalo Charlotte Chicago Cincinnati Cleveland Columbus Dallas Denver Detroit Fort Worth Houston Indianapolis Knoxville Las Vegas Los Angeles Louisville Madison Memphis Miami Milwaukee Minneapolis Nashville New Orleans New York Omaha Orlando Philadelphia Phoenix Pittsburgh Portland Raleigh Richmond Rutherford Sacramento Salt Lake City San Antonio San Diego San Francisco San Jose Seattle Tampa Tucson Washington Log in Sign up Search Search Cancel Search National Today Submit Tucson Today By the People, for the People News Sports Basketball Baseball Body Building Boxing Cycling Equestrian eSports Extreme Sports Football Golf Gymnastics Hockey Ice Skating Mixed Martial Arts Motorsports Olympics Other Sports Rodeo Running Skiing Soccer Softball Surfing Swimming Tennis Track & Field Volleyball Wrestling More Arizona's Koa Peat Makes Impact in Return From Injury Wildcats cruise to 84-61 win over Kansas Jayhawks with Peat back in the lineup. Mar. 2, 2026 at 11:55pm Got story updates? Submit your updates here. › Arizona forward Koa Peat made his return in an emphatic way as the Wildcats cruised to an 84-61 win over the Kansas Jayhawks at home. Peat had missed Arizona's previous three games after suffering a lower body injury against Texas Tech on Feb. 14, but his return has raised the ceiling for one of the best teams in the country heading into the NCAA Tournament. Why it matters Peat's performance against Kansas is a positive sign for Arizona as they look to make a deep run in the NCAA Tournament. The Wildcats were able to survive his absence, but having Peat back at full strength makes them an even more dangerous team. The details Peat scored 12 points on 4-9 shooting, grabbed 7 rebounds, and dished out 5 assists in 23 minutes of action against Kansas. Head coach Tommy Lloyd spoke about the impact of having Peat back on the court, saying it's 'good to kind of get all the pieces back together' as they look to 'reform into something even better down the stretch of the season'. Peat suffered a lower body injury against Texas Tech on Feb. 14. Peat missed Arizona's previous three games before returning against Kansas on Feb. 28, 2026. Arizona has two regular season games remaining against Iowa State and Colorado. The players Koa Peat A freshman forward for the Arizona Wildcats who is averaging 13.8 points and 5.4 rebounds per game this season.
